enriched_points.expanded_content: Robust control ensures systems remain stable and perform optimally, even under varying conditions or uncertainties, as highlighted in control theory frameworks.
Uncertainties can stem from model inaccuracies, external disturbances, and parameter variations, requiring adaptive strategies to maintain system reliability and performance.
Various robust control strategies, including H-infinity and mu-synthesis, are employed to design systems that withstand uncertainties while ensuring desired performance metrics are met.
